title: oxalis acetosella subsp. japonica
intro: Oxalis acetosella subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese wood sorrel, is a subspecies of the common wood sorrel plant, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and edible, tangy leaves. The medicinal properties of Oxalis acetosella subsp. japonica include its high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, inflammation, and as an antibacterial agent. The health benefits of consuming this plant are said to include lowering blood pressure, reducing the risk of kidney stones, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Oxalis acetosella subsp. japonica can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to soothe skin irritations and promote overall well-being.
